title: Tamil Culture and Religion in Sri Lanka and Beyond
further_info: Recommended Reading Allocco, Amy L. 2023. Wonder in the Cremation Ground. The Affective and Transformative Dimensions of an Urban Tamil Festival. In Tulasi Srinivas (ed.), Wonder in South Asia. New York: SUNY Press, pp. 167-195. Arasu, Ponni. 2019. TamilThanmai: A Historical Ethnography of Public Political Lives in Tamilnadu (1950-1970). PhD Thesis, Department of History University of Toronto. Balasundaram, Sasikumar. 2014. An Indentured Tamil Goddess. Mariyamman’s Migration to Ceylon’s Plantations as a Worker. In Sree Padma (ed.), Inventing and Reinventing the Goddess. Lanham et al.: Lexington Books, pp. 103-119. Bass, Daniel and B. Skanthakumar (eds.). 2019. Up-country Tamils: Charting a New Future in Sri Lanka. Colombo: ICES. Daniel, E. Valentine. [1984] 1987. Fluid Signs. Being a Person the Tamil Way. Berkeley/Los Angeles: University of California Press. Jegathesan, Mythri. 2019. Tea and Solidarity. Tamil Women and Work in Postwar Sri Lanka. Seattle: University of Washington. Lawrence, Patricia. 1998. Grief on the Body: The Work of Oracles in Eastern Sri Lanka. In Michael Roberts (ed.), Sri Lanka. Collective Identities Revisited. Volume II. Colombo: Marga Institute, pp. 271-294. Nadarajah, Yaso. 2021. Future Past I am a coolie-al…and I Reside as an Invisible Island inside the Ocean: Tidalectics, Transoceanic Crossings, Coolitude and a Tamil Identity. Island Studies Journal 16 (1): 155-172. Silva, Kalinga Tudor, P.P. Sivapragasam & Thanges Paramsothy (eds.), Casteless or Caste-blind? Dynamics of Concealed Caste Discrimination, Social Exclusion and Protest in Sri Lanka. New Delhi: Indian Institute of Dalit Studies & Kumaran Book House Thiranagama, Sharika. 2011. In My Mother's House. Philadelphia: University of Pennsylvania Press. Whitaker, Mark P. , Darini Rajasingham-Senanayake & Pathmanesan Sanmugeswaran (eds.). 2022. Multi-religiosity in Contemporary Sri Lanka. Innovation, Shared Spaces, Contestations. London/New York: Routledge. online information e-learning course Online seminar Only the first two sessions will be held in person: on Friday, 17 April, and Friday, 24 April, 9 to 11 a.m. (see "scheduled dates") All subsequent sessions will be held online: 4 May to 22 June 2026: Mondays, 11 a.m. to 1 p.m. and Friday, 17 July, and Saturday, 18 July 2026: online block, 9 a.m. to 4.30 p.m. Please register for this course via heiCO and with the instructor: eva.ambos(at)posteo.de
